A1.title: Ball in the pool
A1.notes: Canonical picture-grounded spine for prompt construction. Use the units below as the content plan; do not turn them into an adult-style polished retell.
units: Two animals play near the pool with a ball.
The ball falls into the water.
One animal goes into the pool or reaches in to get it.
The ball is recovered and brought back.
The animals are happy again.
content_invariants: ball or small play object in the water
optional_content: whether the helper swims or uses hands
small background details such as a sign or pool edge
prompt_constraints: Preserve the order of the spine.
Keep the narration child-like and simple.
Do not add adult-style exposition or new plot events.

A2.title: No running
A2.notes: Canonical picture-grounded spine for prompt construction. The story centers the warning sign, the slip, and the bandage.
units: Two animals approach the pool area and a sign warns not to run.
One animal starts to run toward the water or jumping board.
The running animal slips and hurts a knee.
A helper or lifeguard comes with a bandage.
The injured animal sits on a bench and rests.
content_invariants: running or rushing toward the water
optional_content: whether another character points at the sign
whether the helper is a lifeguard or a friend
prompt_constraints: Preserve the order of the spine.
Keep the narration child-like and simple.
Do not add adult-style exposition or new plot events.

A3.title: Airplane in the pool
A3.notes: Canonical picture-grounded spine for prompt construction. The story centers a toy airplane, a pool, and a rescue with a tool.
units: Two animals play with an airplane near the pool.
The airplane is taken or dropped into the water.
The owner gets upset and someone tries to reach it.
A helper with a net or pole recovers the airplane.
The airplane is returned and everyone is happy.
optional_content: whether the helper is a lifeguard, parent, or another adult
whether the retrieval uses a net, pole, or other tool
prompt_constraints: Preserve the order of the spine.
Keep the narration child-like and simple.
Do not add adult-style exposition or new plot events.

B1.title: Sandcastle trouble
B1.notes: Canonical picture-grounded spine for prompt construction. The story is about collaborative building and the castle being ruined.
units: Rabbit and dog build a sandcastle together.
One of them adds sand or dumps a bucket onto the castle.
The sandcastle gets ruined.
The other animal is sad or cries.
They try to fix it or make up.
optional_content: whether the setting is a beach or sandbox
prompt_constraints: Preserve the order of the spine.
Keep the narration child-like and simple.
Do not add adult-style exposition or new plot events.

B2.title: Picnic tummyache
B2.notes: Canonical picture-grounded spine for prompt construction. The story is about a picnic, overeating, and a doctor helping.
units: Rabbit and dog gather food and have a picnic.
One child eats too much.
The child gets a tummyache or feels sick.
A doctor checks the child and helps.
The child feels better and rests or goes home.
optional_content: whether the food is carrots, sandwiches, or berries
whether the child feels dizzy or just sick
whether the ending is resting, sitting, or going home
prompt_constraints: Preserve the order of the spine.
Keep the narration child-like and simple.
Do not add adult-style exposition or new plot events.

B3.title: Balloon trouble
B3.notes: Canonical picture-grounded spine for prompt construction. The story centers a balloon that gets lost and the search for a replacement.
units: Rabbit and dog carry or pull a wagon with a balloon.
The balloon comes loose, floats away, or pops.
The children are upset and look for another balloon.
They go to an adult who has balloons.
Money or price becomes an issue, and the adult helps them get balloons.
The children each get a balloon and are happy.
optional_content: whether the adult is a seller, vendor, or helper
whether a doctor-like adult appears in the scene
the number or color of balloons
whether the balloon comes loose or pops
prompt_constraints: Preserve the order of the spine.
Keep the narration child-like and simple.
Do not add adult-style exposition or new plot events.
